busdesc: A & E Plastik Pak Inc. is a commodity chemicals company. It is headquartered in the United States.

Textron Systems Corporation designs, develops, manufactures, and supports aerospace and defense technologies. It offers unmanned aircraft systems, micro air vehicles, remote video terminals, ground control stations, and portable ground control stations.

A.A. Importing Company, Inc. designs, manufactures, and supplies antique reproductions and furniture items. The company's products are made of wood, iron, porcelain, bronze, crystal, glass, and stained glass.

add1: One AAR Place, 1100 North Wood Dale Road
busdesc: AAR Corp. provides products and services to commercial aviation, government, and defense markets worldwide. It operates through Aviation Services and Expeditionary Services segments.

A.B.A. Industries Inc. was acquired by McSwain Manufacturing Corporation. A.B.A. Industries Inc. manufactures aircraft engine and advanced components for engines and industrial gas turbines. The company is based in Pinellas Park, Florida.
ABC Industries, Inc. manufactures and supplies industrial ventilation products and industrial textile fabrics.
ABKCO Music & Records, Inc. operates as an entertainment company serving customers in the United States and internationally.
ABM Computer Systems Inc. was acquired by Franklin Telecommunications Corp. ABM Computer Systems Inc. is a technology hardware, storage and peripherals company. It is based in Irvine, California.

ABS Industries, Inc., through Colfor and Colmach, operates exclusively in the cold and warm forging industry and the machining of forged components. The company was incorporated in 1979 and is based in Willoughby, Ohio.
